info.name: Labor Project Backend - Local
info.description:
Local Postman collection for Labor Project Backend.
Every request shows its contract explicitly in that request: method, URL, required headers, query params, and body.
Protected requests use Header: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}.
Run Auth > POST /api/auth/login first to get AccessToken and RefreshToken.

variable.description: Local backend base URL for testing.
Local backend WebSocket base URL for Worker Mobile realtime connection.
Bearer access token returned by POST /api/auth/login. Used in Authorization header.
Refresh token returned by POST /api/auth/login. Used by POST /api/auth/refresh body.
Token returned when login gets ACTIVE_SESSION_EXISTS. Used by confirm-force login.
Client device id sent in login body.
Client device name sent in login body.
Gate ClientId used by POST /api/gate/tickets Basic Auth.
Gate ClientSecret shown once by Admin Settings create/rotate Gate client APIs.
Worker code or username used by Admin Workers /:id routes, for example MN000012.
Lower-rank admin account id used by /api/admin/users/{{adminUserId}}/permissions. Set by POST /api/admin/admins.
ticketNo returned by POST /api/gate/tickets. With the Gate payload this equals TicketNo.
marketCode returned by Admin history/operations. With the Gate payload this equals MarketCode.
boothCode used by admin stall APIs and worker ticket completion. With the Gate payload this equals BoothCode.
productCode returned by Gate response and used by worker product quantity confirmation. With the Gate payload this equals ProductCode.
Signed token from the vendor LINE completion message: copy value after action=vendor_confirm_completion&token=.
Signed token from the vendor LINE completion message: copy value after action=vendor_reject_completion&token=.
Driver QR token returned by POST /api/gate/tickets.
Worker QR value returned by POST /api/gate/tickets or WORKER_ASSIGNED. For Gate-created jobs this equals ticketNo and is sent as QrToken when worker checks in.
Driver guest session token returned by POST /api/driver/qr-sessions.
Worker code used by admin assignment APIs.

request.description:
Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/auth/login
Auth: No auth required
Headers: Content-Type: application/json
Query: None
Body: Admin username/password only.
Response: login returns tokens only: AccessToken, RefreshToken, TokenType, ExpiresIn.
Notes: Saves AccessToken and RefreshToken into collection variables when login succeeds. Call GET /api/auth/me after login to fetch admin profile data, permissions, and latest activity.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/auth/login
Auth: No auth required
Headers: Content-Type: application/json
Query: None
Body: Worker username plus password=phone from worker creation, DeviceId, and DeviceName.
Response: login returns tokens only: AccessToken, RefreshToken, TokenType, ExpiresIn.
Notes: Saves AccessToken and RefreshToken into collection variables when login succeeds. If another worker session exists on a different device, saves LoginChallengeToken for POST /api/auth/login/confirm-force. Call GET /api/auth/me after login to fetch worker profile data. After successful worker login, open WS /ws/workers, then call POST /api/workers/me/online.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/auth/login/confirm-force
Auth: No auth required
Headers: Content-Type: application/json
Query: None
Body: JSON: LoginChallengeToken, DeviceId, DeviceName
Notes: Use only after login returns ACTIVE_SESSION_EXISTS with a LoginChallengeToken.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/auth/refresh
Auth: No auth required
Headers: Content-Type: application/json
Query: None
Body: JSON: RefreshToken
Notes: Returns a new AccessToken and RefreshToken.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <AccessToken>.

request.description:
Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/auth/logout
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Query: None
Body: None
Notes: Backend reads session_id from access token and revokes that session.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <AccessToken>.

request.description:
Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/auth/me
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Query: None
Body: None
Notes: Returns current account from access token. Response includes role, FullName, and EmployeeCode for both admin and worker. Admin keeps AdminCode/PermissionLevel/permissions. Worker keeps WorkerCode/profile fields.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <AccessToken>.

request.description:
Method: PATCH
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/auth/me/password
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}, Content-Type: application/json
Query: None
Body: CurrentPassword and NewPassword
Notes: Works for both Admin and Worker. Backend verifies CurrentPassword, hashes NewPassword, keeps the current session active, and revokes other active sessions for the same account.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <AccessToken>.

formdata.description: Optional worker profile image. Allowed: jpg, png, webp. Max 5 MB.
JSON string array. Send 1-2 current shifts for the worker. Backend assigns ShiftNo by array order.

request.description:
Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/users
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}. Do not set Content-Type manually for multipart/form-data; Postman sets the boundary.
Query: None
Body: multipart/form-data from create-worker UI. Optional file field: image. Text fields: FullName, phone, nationality, ShirtType, ShirtNumber, WorkStartDate, WorkSchedules, status.
Response: message only, Worker created successfully.
Notes: Admin only. username defaults to WorkerCode generated from nationality + ShirtType + ShirtNumber, such as MN000012. nationality accepts Myanmar or Cambodia. ShirtType accepts Navy, Blue, or Green. ShirtNumber is unique and cannot be reused. Worker password is always the hashed value of phone, and phone is saved to accounts.phone. WorkSchedules is a JSON string array with 1-2 current shifts; backend assigns ShiftNo by array order. nationality is stored directly in worker_profiles. WorkStartDate is the first working date. Legacy ShiftStartTime/ShiftEndTime or WorkSchedule are still supported. Uploaded image is stored under /uploads/workers and saved as profile.ImageUrl. JSON body with ImageUrl/img is still supported when no file upload is needed.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <AccessToken>.

query.description: Optional. Matches vehicle/market/stall/confirmation/assignment status. Supported examples: WAIT, WORKING, DELIVERED, REJECT, COMPLETED, CANCELLED, PENDING, ACCEPTED, SCANNED, TIMEOUT.

request.description:
Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/users?page=1&limit=20&search=&status=
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Query: page, limit, search, status
Body: None
Notes: Admin only. Lists worker accounts newest first. Response item is trimmed for worker table UI only: WorkerCode, ShirtNumber, FullName, WorkSchedule, WorkSchedules, status, UpdatedAt. Shift availability uses only ShiftStartTime and ShiftEndTime.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <AccessToken>.

request.description:
Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/users/{{userId}}
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Query: None
Body: None
Notes: Admin only. {{userId}} must be WorkerCode or username, for example MN000012. Response is trimmed for worker edit UI: ImageUrl, WorkerCode, FullName, status, and details. details includes phone, position, nationality, ShirtNumber, ShirtType, and all current WorkSchedules.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <AccessToken>.

request.description:
Method: PATCH
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/users/{{userId}}
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}, Content-Type: application/json
Query: None
Body: JSON fields from worker edit UI: ImageUrl/img, WorkerCode, FullName, phone, nationality, status, position, ShirtNumber, ShirtType, WorkStartDate, WorkSchedules. nationality accepts Myanmar or Cambodia. ShirtType accepts Navy, Blue, or Green. ShirtNumber is unique and cannot be reused. WorkSchedules replaces current schedule rows, accepts 1-2 shifts, and backend assigns ShiftNo by array order. work_date, ShiftStartTime/ShiftEndTime, nested profile, and WorkSchedule are still supported for backward compatibility.
Notes: Admin only. {{userId}} must be WorkerCode or username. If nationality, ShirtType, or ShirtNumber is sent without WorkerCode, backend regenerates WorkerCode/username from nationality + ShirtType + ShirtNumber, using existing profile values for fields omitted from the request. status inactive revokes active worker sessions. Password reset remains separate at PATCH /api/admin/users/{{userId}}/password.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <AccessToken>.

request.description:
Method: PATCH
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/users/{{userId}}/password
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}, Content-Type: application/json
Query: None
Body: JSON: NewPassword
Notes: Admin only. {{userId}} must be WorkerCode or username. Resets worker password and revokes worker sessions.

header.description: Required for protected routes. Format: Bearer <AccessToken>.

request.description:
Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/jobs/workers/{{userId}}/work-schedules?page=1&limit=20
Auth: Required via Authorization header
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
Query: page, limit
Body: None
Notes: Admin Jobs. {{userId}} must be WorkerCode or username. Returns the current worker work schedule for job operations.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/jobs/workers/status
Auth: Admin access token
Body: None
Notes: Admin Jobs. Lists only active workers who are online and inside their current shift. Response is shaped for the worker queue board with FullName, WorkerCode, ShirtNumber, ImageUrl, ShiftName, LatestActivityAt, StatusEnteredAt, QueuePosition, and one status only. Data is already sorted by board column order and the real order inside each status. Ready workers use Redis QueuePosition. Board status values: open_app, ready, assigned, working, break.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/jobs/workers/{{userId}}/status/force
Auth: Admin access token
Body: status open_app|ready|break, optional reason
Notes: Admin Jobs. {{userId}} must be WorkerCode or username. open_app removes worker from dispatch queue and shows the worker as open_app, ready puts worker into Redis queue, break starts a configured break, default 15 minutes, and consumes a break count.

query.description: Optional date range start in YYYY-MM-DD.
Optional date range end in YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive.
Optional. If omitted, all matching rows are returned.
Optional page size used only when page is sent.
Search vehicle job ref, license plate, gate transaction ref, market, stall, ticket, or vendor name.

request.description:
Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/vehicle-jobs/operations
Auth: Admin access token with jobs:read permission
Purpose: Vehicle operation board for delivery-truck queue/check-in page. Returns ServerTime, summary counts, OperationStatus, workers with ShiftName, scan progress, markets with DropoffPoint, stalls/tickets with RejectReason, and products.
OperationStatus values: unload_now, waiting_unload, waiting_queue, driver_waiting_queue. Work progress should be read from VehicleJob.Status, Workers[].AssignmentStatus, Markets[].Tickets[].Status, and ConfirmationStatus.

query.description: Optional date range start in YYYY-MM-DD.
Optional date range end in YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive.
Optional. If omitted, all matching rows are returned.
Optional page size used only when page is sent.

request.description:
Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/vehicle-jobs/history?date_from=2026-07-01&date_to=2026-07-07&page=1&limit=20&search=&status=
Auth: Admin access token
Query: date_from/date_to/search/status optional. date is still supported as a legacy single-day filter. status can match vehicle, market, stall, stall confirmation, or worker assignment status. page optional; when page is omitted backend returns all matching rows ordered by latest CreatedAt. When page is sent backend returns that page and pagination metadata.
Supported status examples: WAIT, WORKING, DELIVERED, REJECT, COMPLETED, CANCELLED, PENDING, ACCEPTED, SCANNED, TIMEOUT.
Response: Data[].VehicleJob plus Markets[].Tickets[].Products[] for the Admin history report. Search can match vehicle job ref, license plate, gate transaction ref, market ref/name, stall job ref, ticket no, or stall no.
Notes: Use search with a license plate to see what that vehicle delivered, use market ref/name to see vehicles that delivered to that market, or use stall ref/no to see vehicles and products for that stall in the selected date range.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/vehicle-jobs/{{ticketNo}}/assign-workers
Auth: Admin access token
Body: WorkerCodes array
Notes: Admin manually assigns the vehicle job to selected workers. Workers must be active, must not already have an active assignment, and must currently be ready in the Redis queue. Backend marks them assigned for UI, removes them from ready queue, and starts the 1-minute accept timeout.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/vehicle-jobs/{{ticketNo}}/scan-deadline/extend
Auth: Admin access token
Body: minutes required. Omit WorkerCodes to extend all ACCEPTED workers whose scan deadline has not expired; send WorkerCodes to extend selected workers only.
Example selected worker body: { "minutes": 10, "WorkerCodes": ["MN000115"] }
Response: ticketNo, WorkerQrToken, assignments[].WorkerCode/status/ScanDeadlineAt.
Notes: Extends ScanDeadlineAt and reschedules the background scan timeout for the updated ACCEPTED assignments. Expired assignments are not extended. For Gate-created jobs, WorkerQrToken equals ticketNo and is reused for check-in after extension.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/vehicle-jobs/{{ticketNo}}/workers/{{workerCode}}/assignment/cancel
Auth: Admin access token
Body: optional reason
Notes: Cancels one active worker assignment by ticketNo + WorkerCode, removes that worker from dispatch queue, and shows the worker as open_app. Backend does not auto-fill a replacement worker.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/settings
Auth: Admin access token with settings:read permission
Body: None
Notes: Returns runtime settings used by backend calculations.

Method: PATCH
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/settings
Auth: Admin access token with settings:update permission
Body: Partial JSON is allowed. Updated settings affect new calculations after this request; existing assignment deadlines already saved in DB remain unchanged. WorkerAcceptTimeoutLimit controls how many consecutive accept timeouts are allowed before the worker is taken offline for the shift. WorkerScanWarningBeforeMinutes controls when Admin SSE receives ASSIGNMENT_SCAN_DEADLINE_WARNING for ACCEPTED workers who have not checked in. WorkerScanTeamRemainingMinutes controls the shortened scan window for remaining team members after the first worker checks in. VendorConfirmTimeoutHours auto-confirms first submitted totals after vendor timeout, and VendorReconfirmTimeoutHours does the same after a rejected ticket is resubmitted.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/roles
Auth: Admin access token with roles:read permission
Body: None
Notes: Lists admin permission levels ordered from highest to lowest and includes admin accounts grouped inside each role. Permissions are stored per admin account in DB, not derived from this response.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/gate-clients
Auth: Admin access token with gate_clients:read permission
Body: None
Notes: Lists Gate clients for system-to-system authentication. Response never includes ClientSecret or SecretHash.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/gate-clients
Auth: Admin access token with gate_clients:create permission
Body: name required, optional ClientId and status active|inactive
Notes: Creates a Gate client credential. ClientSecret is shown only once in this response and is saved to {{gateClientSecret}} for Postman testing. Backend stores only SecretHash in DB.

Method: PATCH
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/gate-clients/{{gateClientId}}
Auth: Admin access token with gate_clients:update permission
Body: name and/or status active|inactive
Notes: Activate/disable a Gate client without exposing or changing its secret.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/gate-clients/{{gateClientId}}/secret/rotate
Auth: Admin access token with gate_clients:rotate_secret permission
Body: None
Notes: Generates a new ClientSecret for the same ClientId and replaces the stored SecretHash. The old secret stops working immediately. The new secret is shown only once and saved to {{gateClientSecret}}.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/admins
Auth: Admin access token with admins:create permission
Body: username, password, FullName, optional position/email/phone/status, PermissionLevel owner|manager|supervisor, and permissions array
Notes: Creates role=admin only and saves email/phone to accounts.email/accounts.phone when provided. Seed owner admin can create manager/supervisor. Manager can create supervisor only. No API can create owner because the actor must have a higher PermissionLevel than the new account. Saves created account id to {{adminUserId}}.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/users/{{adminUserId}}/permissions
Auth: Admin access token with permissions:read permission
Body: None
Notes: Use an admin account id, not a worker id. The target admin must have a lower PermissionLevel than the actor. Use GET /api/auth/me to see your own permissions.

Method: PATCH
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/users/{{adminUserId}}/permissions
Auth: Admin access token with permissions:update permission
Body: PermissionLevel, optional status active|inactive, and permissions array
Notes: Updates admin PermissionLevel, status, and permissions, then revokes active sessions of that admin account. Permission is stored per account from DB. The actor must have a higher PermissionLevel rank than both the target admin and the new PermissionLevel. The target admin must login again to receive the latest permissions/status in the token.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/gate/tickets
Auth: Gate Basic Auth using {{gateClientId}} / {{gateClientSecret}}. Create it with Admin Settings > POST /api/admin/gate-clients first.
Headers: Content-Type: application/json
Body: Gate payload, 1 request per ticket/booth/product. Required fields: TicketNo, TicketCreatedAt, BoothCount, MarketCode, MarketName, BoothCode, BoothName, LicensePlate, VehicleTypeCode, VehicleTypeName, ProductCode, ProductName, PackageCode, PackageName, Quantity, Dispatch. Backend derives the internal idempotency key from TicketNo + MarketCode + BoothCode + ProductCode and returns WorkersRequired = 1 for now; worker-count calculation will be added later.
Response: Result, Ticket, Market, Booth, Product, and Qr. Ticket includes TicketNo, TicketCreatedAt, BoothCount, vehicle info, WorkersRequired, and Status. Ticket.Status is derived from Dispatch only: true = unload_now, false = waiting_unload. Success/replay responses do not include Message or GateTransactionRef. Qr.WorkerQrToken equals TicketNo, so Worker scans/sends TicketNo for check-in. GateTransactionRef is returned only in 409 mismatch errors for debugging.
Result: 201 + Result CREATED for a new Gate transaction, 200 + Result REPLAYED when the same TicketNo + MarketCode + BoothCode + ProductCode is retried with the same payload, 409 when the same key is reused with a different payload.
Notes: Saves gateResult, ticketNo, marketCode, boothCode, productCode, driverQrToken, and workerQrToken collection variables. Sending the same TicketNo with a new MarketCode/BoothCode/ProductCode appends or updates that market/booth/product under the same vehicle job. With Dispatch true, backend creates or marks the job as WORKING and immediately dispatches ready workers through WebSocket.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/driver/qr-sessions
Auth: No auth required
Body: JSON QrToken from driver QR
Notes: Creates a 24-hour driver guest session and saves driverSessionToken.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/driver/jobs/current
Auth: Driver guest session via Authorization: Bearer {{driverSessionToken}}
Body: None

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/driver/jobs/{{ticketNo}}/ready
Auth: Driver guest session
Body: None
Response: ticketNo, LicensePlate, status, WorkerQrToken.
Notes: Marks vehicle job WORKING and dispatches ready workers from Redis FIFO queue. For Gate-created jobs, WorkerQrToken equals ticketNo and is the value Worker sends as QrToken to check in.

item.name: Worker Application
item.description: WebSocket setup: Postman HTTP request items cannot open ws:// as a native WebSocket tab. Create it manually with New > WebSocket, then use {{wsBaseUrl}}/ws/workers?token={{accessToken}} and click Connect. Do this after POST /api/auth/login - Worker and before POST /api/workers/me/online. WORKER_CONNECTED payload returns WorkerCode, not internal account_id. The socket only confirms the worker realtime connection is alive; the worker becomes online/ready after POST /api/workers/me/online.

request.description:
Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/online
Auth: Worker access token
Body: None
Response: FullName, WorkerCode, status, TodayJobCount, BreakCountUsed, CompletedJobCount.
Notes: Requires active /ws/workers WebSocket. From open_app, worker can enter queue only once per shift/day. If current status is break, worker can call this endpoint to return early before break timer ends. Returns 403 if the worker has no current schedule or is outside the assigned shift. OUTSIDE_WORK_SHIFT includes shift and remaining_time text for the mobile modal.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/offline
Auth: Worker access token
Body: None
Response: FullName, WorkerCode, status, TodayJobCount, BreakCountUsed, CompletedJobCount. The worker is removed from Redis queue internally, response status is open_app, and the current shift is closed so the worker cannot re-enter queue in the same shift.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/break
Auth: Worker access token
Body: None
Response: FullName, WorkerCode, status, BreakCountUsed, BreakCountLimit. status is break.
Notes: Puts worker into break status for the configured duration, default 15 minutes. Requires worker to be ready and inside the current shift time. OUTSIDE_WORK_SHIFT includes shift and remaining_time text for the mobile modal. Default max is 4 breaks per shift from WorkerBreakLimit. BullMQ returns worker to the tail of Redis queue automatically when break ends; worker can call /online early while status is break.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/status
Auth: Worker access token
Body: None
Response: FullName, WorkerCode, ImageUrl, status, nationality, WorkStartDate, phone, and shift name/start/end. status is one of open_app, ready, assigned, working, break. When status is break, response also includes BreakUntil and RemainingBreakTime with TotalSeconds, minutes, seconds, and text.

query.description: Assignment created date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

request.description:
Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/assignments/history?date=2026-07-07
Auth: Worker access token
Query: date required, YYYY-MM-DD
Body: None
Response: date and data[].ticketNo/GateTransactionRef/LicensePlate/status/AcceptDeadlineAt/ScanDeadlineAt/AcceptedAt/ScannedAt/CompletedAt/TimeoutReason/CreatedAt/UpdatedAt.
Notes: Returns assignment history for the current worker only. The date is interpreted in Asia/Bangkok time and filters by assignment CreatedAt. TimeoutReason is null unless status is TIMEOUT; accept_timeout means the worker did not accept in time, scan_timeout means the worker accepted but did not scan QR in time.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/assignments/{{ticketNo}}/accept
Auth: Worker access token
Body: None
Path: {{ticketNo}} comes from WORKER_ASSIGNED.ticketNo.
Notes: Assignment must be PENDING and accepted within WorkerAcceptDeadlineSeconds, default 60 seconds. If not accepted in time, assignment becomes TIMEOUT and the connected worker returns to the tail of the queue. After WorkerAcceptTimeoutLimit consecutive accept timeouts, default 3, the worker is taken offline for the shift. Response is trimmed for the worker job detail screen only: LicensePlate, team[].FullName/WorkerCode/ImageUrl/ScanStatus, markets[].marketName/StallCount/stalls[].boothCode/boothName/ProductCount/products[].productCode/productName/quantity/packageName. Use stalls[].boothCode later with POST /api/workers/me/tickets/{{boothCode}}/complete.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/assignments/{{ticketNo}}/check-in-qr
Auth: Worker access token
Path: {{ticketNo}} comes from WORKER_ASSIGNED.ticketNo.
Body: JSON QrToken scanned from Worker QR. For Gate-created jobs this value is ticketNo.
Response: status, WorkerCode, ticketNo, WorkerQrToken. Normally { "status": "SCANNED", "WorkerCode": "MN000142", "ticketNo": "TKT-20260723-000125", "WorkerQrToken": "TKT-20260723-000125" }.
Notes: Checks worker into the job only while ScanDeadlineAt is still valid. Default scan window comes from WorkerScanDeadlineMinutes after accept. Admin SSE receives ASSIGNMENT_SCAN_DEADLINE_WARNING when an ACCEPTED worker has not checked in and remaining scan time is below WorkerScanWarningBeforeMinutes. In a multi-worker team, once the first worker checks in, remaining ACCEPTED workers receive ASSIGNMENT_SCAN_DEADLINE_SHORTENED and their ScanDeadlineAt is shortened by WorkerScanTeamRemainingMinutes from that first check-in. If scan time expires by API call or background timeout, the assignment becomes TIMEOUT and worker status returns to open_app. Admin can extend ScanDeadlineAt and the worker can retry using the same ticketNo QR value if the assignment is still ACCEPTED. The returned WorkerQrToken is the same ticketNo QR value that was scanned and can be rendered by the mobile app so other assigned workers can scan from this worker screen. If checked-in workers reach workers_required, vehicle job moves to WORKING.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/workers/me/tickets/{{boothCode}}/complete
Auth: Worker access token
Body: Items required. Send every ProductCode in the ticket with ConfirmedQuantity counted by the worker.
Notes: {{boothCode}} is the boothCode from Gate payload/response, such as STALLJOB-001. Any worker mapped to this ticket can submit after the assigned team has checked in, and stalls/markets in the same vehicle job can be submitted in any order. The first request while the ticket is WAIT/WORKING/REJECT succeeds. The ticket and AssignmentStatus become DELIVERED, and vendor is notified via LINE queue with signed confirm/reject tokens. If vendor rejects, ticket and AssignmentStatus become REJECT so worker must resubmit. If vendor does not respond, backend auto-confirms after VendorConfirmTimeoutHours for first submission or VendorReconfirmTimeoutHours after a rejected resubmission. When LINE_DEBUG_POSTBACK_RESPONSE=true, response includes DebugLinePostback and this request saves vendorConfirmToken/vendorRejectToken automatically.

Method: GET
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/admin/events
Auth: Admin access token
Headers: Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}, Accept: text/event-stream
Body: None
Notes: This is an Admin-only SSE stream, not a JSON list. It stays open and receives realtime dashboard events including ASSIGNMENT_SCAN_DEADLINE_WARNING when an ACCEPTED worker has not checked in and remaining scan time is below WorkerScanWarningBeforeMinutes. Snapshot/latest state should still be loaded from Admin REST APIs.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/line/webhook
Auth: No auth. LINE signature is required only when LINE_CHANNEL_SECRET is configured.
Body: Mock LINE postback. userId must match vendor_line_id. Copy vendorConfirmToken from the LINE completion message token= value. Confirms the waiting ticket completion and notifies worker/admin. If this confirmation makes every ticket in the vehicle job terminal, backend closes the market/vehicle job and sets AssignmentStatus to COMPLETED so workers can go online for the next job. If there is another stall job, AssignmentStatus returns to WORKING.

Method: POST
URL: {{baseUrl}}/api/line/webhook
Auth: No auth. LINE signature is required only when LINE_CHANNEL_SECRET is configured.
Body: Mock LINE postback. userId must match vendor_line_id. Copy vendorRejectToken from the LINE completion message token= value. Optional RejectReason is saved to the stall job and returned by Admin operations/history until the worker resubmits. Rejects the waiting ticket completion, sets AssignmentStatus to REJECT, then the worker can submit the ticket completion again.
